key_insight: GrapNet turns the neural network architecture itself into a programmable graph, so relations can be edited, frozen, grouped, routed, or lowered without ad-hoc parameter surgery.
engineering_relevance: This gives engineers a more auditable and controllable substrate for dynamic models, especially when structural changes and backend swaps must preserve faithful execution.
method: Child-owned nodes store next-layer references plus aligned trainable allocation vectors.
Structural rules and execution policies are external to the node core, enabling grow/freeze/edit/group/routing operations.
The same graph can interface with dense layers, CNNs, ResNets, attention blocks, and transformers via a vector-valued parent interface.
evidence_signal: In matched 10-seed studies, GrapNet+ER improved Split Fashion-MNIST seen-class accuracy to 63.16% versus 51.08% for a larger dense MLP+ER, and improved a frozen-ResNet Split CIFAR-10 head by 3.81 points (p=0.0026).
watch_item: Because deletions physically alter graph topology and execution views can change, implementation must carefully preserve consistency between structure, allocation vectors, and training state.
paper_title: GrapNet: A Programmable Dynamic-Architecture Neural Graph Substrate
